editorial_theme: Mother's Day weekend + a stacked Friday night. The Knick keeps rolling.

headliner.name: Greg Piccolo & Heavy Juice — 75th Birthday Celebration
headliner.selection_reason: Local saxophone legend (Roomful of Blues) celebrates his 75th with a brand-new album pre-release. The Knick post-renovation narrative continues. Ticks every Signal box: local hero, milestone moment, story to tell, real venue history.

lineup.name: Salt Marsh Opera presents Cinderella
lineup.raw_description: Salt Marsh Opera performs Gioachino Rossini's classic — operatic enchantment in the intimate Black Box.

lineup.name: Paul Gilbert with Greg Koch
lineup.raw_description: Mr. Big guitarist Paul Gilbert announces a new album WROC, joined by guitar virtuoso Greg Koch. VIP experience sales ending soon.

lineup.name: The Psychology of Serial Killers — Dr. Rachel Toles
lineup.raw_description: Live show by clinical psychologist Dr. Rachel Toles unpacking the psychology of serial killers.

lineup.name: Led Zeppelin — Houses of the Holy (Tribute)
lineup.raw_description: Note-for-note recreation of Houses of the Holy at the Courthouse Center.

lineup.name: In Lieu of Flowers
lineup.raw_description: Indie band at the all-ages Pump House. $15 advance / $20 at the door.

lineup.name: Kingston Hill Gardeners Annual Plant Sale
lineup.raw_description: Perennials cultivated by members. Mother's Day-weekend tradition.

lineup.name: RI Grown Farmers Market — Fishermen's Memorial
lineup.raw_description: Sunday farmers market — early-season plants, flowers, RI seafood, spring vegetables, honey, maple syrup. Mother's Day morning.

spotlight.name: Weekapaug Inn
spotlight.location_detail: 25 Spray Rock Rd, on Quonochontaug Pond
spotlight.occasion: Mother's Day Brunch — Sunday, May 10, 10:30 AM – 2:30 PM, $95/person
spotlight.raw_description: Family-style brunch with waterfront views at the iconic Weekapaug Inn. $95/person, reservations required.
spotlight.selection_reason: Mother's Day is the dominant seasonal beat this issue; Weekapaug is the most South-County-y option for the day. Pricey but the iconic local pick.

signal_noise_candidates.question: Mother's Day Mom Test — where do you actually take her?
signal_noise_candidates.rationale: Mother's Day brunch is a Rhode Island sport. Strong local debate fuel.

signal_noise_candidates.question: Friday May 8 dilemma: which one are you actually going to?
options: Psychology of Serial Killers at United
signal_noise_candidates.rationale: Three legitimately strong shows on the same night. Genuine FOMO.

dropped.name: Annie Hartnett Author Talk (Thu)
dropped.reason: Solid weeknight pick but Salt Marsh Opera is the stronger Thursday entry; can't have two arts events on the same weeknight

dropped.name: Surf & Sand Pottery Painting Workshop (Fri)
dropped.reason: Friday is already overloaded; saved for a slower week

dropped.name: Leonie Swann Author Talk (Sat)
dropped.reason: Quirky and we want it back later, but Saturday is full and we needed outdoor/nightlife instead

dropped.name: A Band Called PoP (Sat)
dropped.reason: Already at 2 events per venue at the Knick (counting Greg Piccolo)

dropped.name: Speakeasy Choir (Sat)
dropped.reason: Charming community angle but third arts event from United on Saturday — diversity cap

dropped.name: Plein Air Painting at Kinney Azalea Gardens (Sat)
dropped.reason: Beautiful but overlaps with the plant sale; chose plant sale for the Mother's Day-gift angle

dropped.name: CVRRENT Film Fest at Surf Exchange (Sat)
dropped.reason: Newer venue, low confidence on draw, lineup already full

dropped.name: Wickford on the Water Mother's Day Brunch (Sun)
dropped.reason: Solid alternative; mention in spotlight body if needed but Weekapaug is the splurge pick

dropped.name: Speaker Series: Gilbert Stuart (Tue)
dropped.reason: Niche local-history; weeknight slot used elsewhere. Worth its own deep cut later.

tease_next_week: Misquamicut Drive-In opens Friday May 15 with Jaws — pure Signal material
Dance Night at the Knick with Eight to the Bar (album release) — May 15
Dirty Dozen Brass Band at Greenwich Odeum — May 14
